title: Hemipilia pugeensis
taxonomy: Hemipilia pugeensis is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This family is commonly known as the orchid family and is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. As a member of this family, Hemipilia pugeensis is further classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae. Within the subfamily Orchidoideae, Hemipilia pugeensis is classified within the tribe Orchideae. Further classification places Hemipilia pugeensis within the genus Hemipilia, which is a small genus of orchids.

intro: Hemipilia pugeensis, also known as Puge Hemipilia, is a species of orchid native to China. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments including infections and injuries. The bioactive constituents of Hemipilia pugeensis include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Hemipilia pugeensis is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, although it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
